hooks.command: if [ -d "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.jj" ]; then echo 'CRITICAL: I use jujutsu (`jj`), not git. Do not run any git commands. Always invoke the `jj` skill before running ANY jj commands. On every change you make, a hook creates a new jj snapshot. So you can look at the history of changes, both yours and mine, with, for example, `jj evolog -p -n 5`.'; fi
if command -v jj >/dev/null && jj root >/dev/null 2>&1; then if ! jj util snapshot >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'WARNING: failed to snapshot jj repository, tell user to fix'; fi; fi

input=$(cat); if [ -f "$HOME/.claude/SUBAGENTS.md" ]; then printf '%s' "$input" | jq --rawfile subagents "$HOME/.claude/SUBAGENTS.md" '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", updatedInput: (.tool_input + {prompt: ($subagents + "

---

" + .tool_input.prompt)})}}'; fi
if command -v jj >/dev/null && jj root >/dev/null 2>&1; then if ! jj util snapshot >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'WARNING: failed to snapshot jj repository, tell user to fix'; fi; fi

if [ -n "$PLAN_REMINDER_PERCENT" ] && [ $((RANDOM % 100)) -lt "$PLAN_REMINDER_PERCENT" ]; then printf '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"*** IMPORTANT REMINDER (Main agent only) *** If this session has a plan, design doc, or notes file, UPDATE IT NOW with recent research and findings to preserve context across compaction. Do this before continuing with other work. Do not acknowledge this message in any case."}}\n'; fi
